quoteFirst: It was an energetic summons to rise and view a fresh and beautiful world, and Billie, glancing at her watch, was aware that, as a concession to new arrivals, the summons had come half an hour later than scheduled.
quoteLast: was to be the hour for rising in camp, provided the ladies were willing. And certainly they showed no signs of unwillingness at the six o'clock call.
title: The Motor Maids at Sunrise Camp
author: Stokes, Katherine

quoteFirst: They all wanted to wait till the moon should be high in the sky before starting.  We first struck across the plain of pasture and scrub to get back to the track, and then pursued our way along it eastwards.  At
quoteLast: we saw some tents to the south, but these our guides said were the wrong ones.  An hour later met two zellems, who told us the contrary, but too late for us to return; and they added that they came from a camp of Salamat Arabs an hour or so further east.
title:
A Pilgrimage to Nejd, the Cradle of the Arab Race. Vol. 2 [of 2]
A Visit to the Court of the Arab Emir, and "our Persian Campaign."
author: Blunt, Anne, Lady, 1837-1917; Blunt, Wilfrid Scawen, 1840-1922 [Editor]

title: Children's book of patriotic stories: The spirit of '76
author: Dickinson, Asa Don, 1876-1960 [Editor]; Dickinson, Helen Winslow Dickinson, 1885-1950 [Editor]

quoteLast: we made Larchmont. The club-house piazza was crowded with gold braid, yachting-caps, and booze. Wigwagged that we were the Good Ship Lithia, and they signalled back, “Look out for floating mines.
title: The Log of the Water Wagon; or, The Cruise of the Good Ship "Lithia"
author: Taylor, Bert Leston, 1866-1921; Gibson, W. C. (William Curtis), 1857-; Glackens, L. M. (Louis M.), 1866-1933 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: At nine we were obliged to land in order to gum the canoe, when the weather became extremely warm.  Numerous tracks of rein-deer appeared on the side of the river.  At
quoteLast: we took our station for the night, and set the nets.  The current was very strong all day, and we found it very difficult to walk along the beach, from the large stones which were scattered over it.
title: Voyages from Montreal Through the Continent of North America to the Frozen and Pacific Oceans in 1789 and 1793. Vol. I
author: Mackenzie, Alexander, 1764-1820

quoteFirst: At six he was dead, and the doctor testified he must have been dead between fifteen and thirty minutes when he was called in. So that fixes the time of the shooting between
quoteLast: and six. Marchburn expected a woman to call upon him that night, because he would not have made such careful preparations for secrecy if his visitor had been a man. He did not want his clerks to see his caller. The time between her calling and the shooting was too short for them to have quarreled; but it was long enough for her to have made her demand and to have been refused by Marchburn. Then she shot him.
title: The Black Cat, Vol. I, No. 6, March 1896
author: Various

quoteFirst: "Thursday, April 1st. Fine weather. Getting ready for sea. At
quoteLast: A.M. up kedge and weighed and turned out of ye cove. The Cumberland got under way and proceeded down the harbour. At 8 A.M. (We having the Cumberland in company) cleared the heads of Port Jackson, and at half-past 8 parted with Cumberland, leaving her with a fine moderate breeze at south-south-east, and by half-past 9 she bore Sydney 4 or 5 miles. By 11 A.M. got abreast of Barren Jowie* (* Barrenjoey.
title:
The Logbooks of the Lady Nelson
With the journal of her first commander Lieutenant James Grant
author: Lee, Ida, 1865-1943

quoteFirst: I was glad to leave London in Canada West for that reason, and departed the next day in a fresh waggon at
quoteLast: , arriving at the Corners, six miles off, where a bran-new settlement and bran-new toll-gate appeared with a fine cross road, that to the right leading to Westminster, that to the left to Lake Erie. I was sorry that the plank road was finished only to this place; but we had fine settlements all the way.
title: Canada and the Canadians, Vol. 2
author: Bonnycastle, Richard Henry, Sir, 1791-1847

quoteFirst: When I arrived there, I found awaiting me a telegram from Sir Robert Detling asking me to call on him without delay. I set forth at once and arrived at Sir Robert’s house at
quoteLast: , and was shown into his study immediately. Sir Robert then told me that he had come to the conclusion that the circumstances of my brother’s death called for some investigation and that he proposed to communicate with the coroner. He urged me not to raise any objections and advised me to say nothing to any one but to wait until the coroner’s decision was made known.
title: As a thief in the night
author: Freeman, R. Austin (Richard Austin), 1862-1943

title:
West Point
An Intimate Picture of the National Military Academy and of the Life of the Cadet
author: Richardson, Robert C. (Robert Charlwood), 1882-1954; Scott, Hugh Lenox, 1853-1934 [Author of introduction, etc.]
